normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def knap (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat) : Nat := 0
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the function to be specified (placeholder).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat), w.length = v.length → 0 ≤ cap
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume equal-length weight/value lists and non-negative capacity (Nat already ensures non-negativity).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat), w.length = v.length → (∀ x ∈ w, True) ∧ (∀ x ∈ v, True)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Weights and values are non-negative (Nat already ensures non-negativity); no additional constraints beyond membership.
Characterize feasible solutions as subsets of indices within bounds whose total weight is ≤ capacity.
The returned value is at least the value of any feasible subset (so it is an upper bound on all feasible subset values).
There exists a feasible subset whose total value equals the returned maximum.
Equate the result with the maximum total value over all feasible subsets (maximality characterization).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
∀ (w v : List Nat) (cap : Nat) (S : Finset Nat), w.length = v.length →
  (∀ i ∈ S, i < w.length) →
  let totalV := S.sum (fun i => v.get ⟨i, by simpa using (Fin.isLt i (by simpa using ‹i < w.length›))⟩)
  True
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Each item can be selected at most once, modeled by using a set of indices (Finset) rather than multisets.
